hard.opp_name: Signet Jewelers_CLM Merlin, Merlin Intake, Spend Analytics
hard.next_step: Dry-run demo focused on Intake + CLM for Erin, Megan, and DeVonn before General Counsel review: 6/24/2026 Signet to share workflow diagrams and sample NDA template for a tailored demo. Completed Zycus to send pricing/budgetary questionnaire. sent 6/23/2026 Zycus to provide budgetary proposal within 1–2 weeks. Align demo around SAP, Workday, and Adobe Sign integrations. Prepare follow-up discussion on Spend Analytics, Catalog, and eSourcing as next-phase opportunities.
hard.ais_why:
• Signet's core problems are 'manual workflows, lack of visibility, and no linkage between contract data and spend' ‚Äî the driver is CLM and spend analytics rather than AI.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped and a dry-run demo focused on 'Intake + CLM' is planned, showing openness to Zycus AI-enabled modules.
• Agentic-platform-discussed is not set; interest is real but centered on fixing CLM pain, so appetite is curious-not-hungry.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live, fast-moving CLM replacement: the 24 Jun dry-run workshop landed strongly, the recording was pushed to the VP and General Counsel, and Rick is booking the pricing discussion; the sole EARLY-stage gap is that no economic buyer or legal decision-maker has yet joined a call.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 18 Jun 2026, Qualified since 19 Jun; ~17 days in Qualified as of 6 Jul. Days to close 165 (close 18 Dec 2026), and the buyer pulled the close in from 16 Jun 2027 to 18 Dec 2026 on 23 Jun (field history) which tightens the clock. Buyer's own internal decision target is August 2026 with September budget season, so the real forcing clock (~6 to 8 weeks) is tighter than the SF close date. Last activity 2 Jul (4 days ago); three buyer touchpoints since the 21 Oct 2025 intro, two in the last 18 days. Pace is healthy for the stage.
evidence: CLM Workshop 24 Jun 2026 delivered as the explicit dry-run before General Counsel review; Erin Shehadi said 'I love this' and Megan Snyder validated the parallel-review and Microsoft Word plug-in for legal.
Erin Shehadi email 26 Jun 2026: 'The link isn't working... I also want to send it to my VP and our General Counsel' (Clari inbound, Task 26 Jun).
Recording access extended 2 Jul 2026 to matthew.widdoes, Mark.Peasley and Kim.Raines at SignetJewelers.com, widening the internal audience (Task 2 Jul).
Rick Hunsinger email 2 Jul 2026: 'Let's connect next week and schedule the pricing discussion' (Task 2 Jul).
Buyer-owned compelling event: the incumbent CLM (GEP) is sunsetting with a hard deadline of September 2027 per Erin Shehadi (Avoma workshop 24 Jun).

metrics.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated but not yet quantified. On the 24 Jun workshop Erin Shehadi confirmed roughly 80% of contracts run on third-party supplier paper that legal reviews manually ('anything over 200,000 goes to procurement... those are about 80 percent of the contracts, and those are on third party paper'), and that policies live as scattered Word documents on an intranet with no system guidance. No cycle-time, contract-volume, compliance-incident, or resource-hour figures have been captured to anchor ROI. Zycus sent a sizing questionnaire on 23 Jun (annual contract volume, power/business users, historical contracts, intake requestors, annual spend) that will produce the inputs, so quantifying the cost of the manual/GEP-sunset baseline is a ranked move to harden the budgetary proposal.
sources: Clari email 'Zycus Questions for budgetary estimate' 23 Jun 2026

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget approver has not been engaged. The working team (Erin Shehadi, Megan Snyder, DeVonn Nixon) sits at Manager / Sr. Manager level in Procurement Technology and Enablement and does not own a $350k budget. The strongest live signal is Erin's 26 Jun email naming, but not introducing, 'my VP and our General Counsel', and the 2 Jul extension of recording access to the relevant stakeholder, Mark Peasley and Kim Raines, one of whom may be the VP but none confirmed. Inferred from the conversation, the VP (Erin's manager) is the economic buyer and the General Counsel is the legal sign-off, but neither is named in Salesforce or on a call. Securing the VP's name, a warm introduction, and budget-envelope validation before September budget season is the single highest-leverage gap.
sources: Clari email received from Erin Shehadi 26 Jun 2026

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the 24 Jun workshop, run directly against Signet's own requirement and workflow document. Confirmed must-haves: a Teams-native intake front door so users do not learn a new tool (Megan Snyder: 'I do like the convenience of this being within Teams'), a Microsoft Word plug-in that removes check-in/check-out for legal redlining, confidentiality applied from the request/intake stage, parallel review routing of clauses to named reviewers, a central clause and template library, AI review of third-party paper (Instareview) and automated executive-summary generation, role-based access for ~2,000 potential requesters, reporting/dashboards, and eventual SAP vendor-master and Adobe Sign integration. Erin also flagged orchestration/interconnectivity across modules as the key differentiator she is testing us on.
sources: SF Next_Step__c (SAP, Workday, Adobe Sign integrations)

decision_process.narrative: The process is becoming visible from the call sequence: intro (21 Oct 2025) then discovery (18 Jun 2026) then the dry-run Intake+CLM workshop for the working team (24 Jun) explicitly framed to prepare a sharper tailored demo for the legal team and General Counsel, then internal review of the shared recording, then VP/budget review. The buyer's stated internal decision target is August 2026 ahead of the September budget season, and the 24 Jun takeaways note Signet intends to run a formal CLM-focused RFP, which may sit as a gate between now and selection. General Counsel review is a named legal gate. The approval chain above the working team (VP for budget, GC for legal) is identified by role but not yet engaged, so the process map is real but the power tier is still unconfirmed.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are emerging. A mutual NDA is in motion: Megan Snyder shared Signet's MNDA template and workflow on 18 Jun, and Signet is to share workflow diagrams and a sample NDA template for the tailored demo (Next_Step). General Counsel review is the explicit legal gate before any agreement. Signet's current e-signature tool is Adobe Sign, and Zycus offers a pre-built Adobe Sign integration plus its own Certinal e-sign, which de-risks that step. As a full-suite new-business CLM deal this will need an MSA, Order Form and SOW, none of which has surfaced yet. The incumbent GEP CLM's September 2027 sunset is the practical forcing function; pricing paper begins once the budgetary proposal lands.
sources: Clari email 'Follow-Up: MNDA Template & Workflow' 18 Jun 2026

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and owner-attributed. On 24 Jun the buyer confirmed the incumbent CLM (GEP) is sunsetting with a hard September 2027 deadline; intake is manual with no single front door; confidentiality is not applied at the request stage ('when requests are submitted... everybody can see it, and then we have to flip it as confidential' per Erin Shehadi); policies live as disparate Word documents on an intranet, so ~80% of contracts that bypass procurement get no policy guidance; and legal manually reviews the ~80% of contracts on third-party paper. The pain owners are the Procurement Technology and Enablement team (Erin Shehadi, Megan Snyder, DeVonn Nixon) and, downstream, the Legal/General Counsel team carrying the redline workload. What remains inferred is the hours and cycle-time lost, not yet quantified.

champion.narrative: Erin Shehadi (Sr. Manager, Procurement Technology and Enablement) is the developing champion: she owns scheduling and the proposal conversation, drove requirements throughout the 24 Jun workshop, and on 26 Jun proactively moved to push the recording up to her VP and General Counsel, clear internal-selling behaviour. Megan Snyder (Manager, Procurement Technology and Enablement) is an active co-champion who supplied the MNDA template and workflow and pressed detailed CLM and executive-summary requirements. What has developed: ownership of next steps and stated intent to escalate to power. What is still missing: a confirmed warm introduction to the VP and General Counsel, and an explicit commitment to advocate in those rooms. The champions are engaged and warm, not yet senior or power-connected.
sources: Clari email received from Erin Shehadi 26 Jun 2026
Clari email 'Follow-Up: MNDA Template & Workflow' from Megan Snyder 18 Jun 2026

competition.narrative: No named external software rival has entered head-to-head yet, but the 24 Jun workshop reveals the incumbent CLM is GEP: Erin Shehadi asked whether the supplier record is 'a different module that we buy with GEP', and the sunsetting platform Signet must replace by September 2027 is that incumbent. The live threats are therefore GEP (incumbent, being displaced, but a candidate to re-compete if it enters the planned formal CLM RFP) and Do Nothing / defer to the next fiscal year on budget grounds. Our wedge is the interconnected single-platform story Erin herself praised as the differentiator ('you all lack that interconnectivity... those data silo gaps that the system fully integrated on one platform doesn't have'): Merlin Teams intake plus CLM plus Instareview AI for third-party paper. We win by landing the legal-friendly budgetary proposal and shaping the RFP criteria before the field opens.
sources: Prior canonical record (Do Nothing / sunsetting incumbent)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is building week over week: the dry-run workshop landed 24 Jun, the recording was shared and then pushed to VP/GC and three more senior names, and Rick is now booking the pricing discussion. The buyer also pulled the close date in by six months on 23 Jun. The one thing that has NOT moved is direct engagement with the economic buyer or General Counsel.

items.change: Buyer pulled close date in from 16 Jun 2027 to 18 Dec 2026
Zycus sent budgetary/sizing questionnaire for the pricing proposal
Dry-run Intake + CLM workshop delivered to Erin, Megan, DeVonn as prep for the General Counsel demo
Erin Shehadi requested recording access to loop in her VP and General Counsel
Shared Space created and recording shared to Erin, Megan, DeVonn
Recording access extended to Matthew Widdoes, Mark Peasley, Kim Raines; Rick to schedule pricing discussion

competitive_position.summary: No named software rival is in a head-to-head yet. The strongest CURRENT threats are timing/budget (deferral to next fiscal year) and the incumbent GEP CLM, which Signet is replacing because it is sunsetting by September 2027, a forcing event in our favour but also a vendor that could re-compete if invited to the planned formal CLM RFP. The priority is to shape that RFP's criteria and land the ROI-anchored proposal before September budget season, so no competitor is invited in on price.

competitors.how_we_win: Lean on GEP's September 2027 sunset as the compelling event; present the interconnected single-platform CLM plus Merlin intake plus Instareview story Erin praised, and land the proposal before any formal RFP re-invites GEP on price.

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of continuing manually (legal hours on 80% third-party paper, cycle time, confidentiality exposure) so deferring past the GEP sunset is more expensive than proceeding; secure VP budget sponsorship.

competitors.name: Future formal CLM RFP (vendors TBD)
competitors.quote: Signet will review recording internally and proceed with formal CLM-focused RFP
competitors.source: Avoma CLM Workshop 24 Jun 2026 key takeaways
competitors.how_we_win: Shape the RFP criteria now around Merlin Teams intake, Instareview AI for third-party paper, and single-platform orchestration before other vendors are invited.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on the core CLM and intake requirements the buyer put in front of us; the working team repeatedly reacted positively during the 24 Jun workshop. The one open exposure is a couple of unconfirmed configuration answers (URL-fed policies, Signet-branded notification domain) and the future SAP vendor-master sync.

best_practice_check.summary: The working-team read is strong; the win now hinges on reaching power before September budget season and pricing the value, not on more feature depth.
flags: Convert the workshop enthusiasm into a live General Counsel and VP session before September budget season; Erin has offered the intro but neither has met us.
Anchor the budgetary proposal to a quantified cost-of-manual and GEP-sunset baseline so deferring past September 2027 is more expensive than buying now.
Shape the planned formal CLM RFP criteria around Merlin Teams intake, Instareview AI and single-platform orchestration before other vendors (including GEP) are invited in.

critical_signals.text: The 24 Jun dry-run CLM workshop landed strongly: Erin Shehadi said 'I love this' and Megan Snyder validated the parallel-review routing and the Microsoft Word legal plug-in.
Signet extended recording access to a VP, the General Counsel and three more senior names (Matthew Widdoes, Mark Peasley, Kim Raines) between 26 Jun and 2 Jul, but none has met us yet.
The incumbent CLM is GEP and it is sunsetting by September 2027, which is our forcing event, though Signet plans a formal CLM RFP that could re-invite GEP or other vendors.
A budgetary proposal is owed within one to two weeks but the ROI is still unquantified and the VP who owns the budget has not validated any envelope.
The buyer wants a phased approach, starting with CLM and Merlin Intake and deferring Spend Analytics, Catalog and eSourcing to a later phase.

champion_strength.summary: Erin Shehadi is a developing champion who owns next steps and proactively moved to push the recording to her VP and General Counsel on 26 Jun; Megan Snyder co-champions at working level. Neither has yet delivered a warm introduction to power or legal.
champion_strength.source: Clari email received from Erin Shehadi 26 Jun 2026 + Avoma CLM Workshop 24 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin Teams intake agent and Instareview AI for third-party paper were front and centre in the 24 Jun workshop and drew the buyer's strongest reactions, so the AI story is well positioned even though the deal driver is CLM pain, not AI appetite.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5 (Moderate) reads accurately: the buyer is genuinely engaged by Merlin intake and AI redlining/Instareview but frames them as means to fix CLM pain rather than an AI mandate. One buyer-stated top priority is a single AI intake use case, so appetite is curious, not hungry.

items.detail: Neither the VP nor the General Counsel decision-maker has joined a call; the deal lives entirely at working level.
ROI is unquantified and the budget envelope is unvalidated; the buyer's own budget season is September for next fiscal year.
Signet has ~2,000 people who can raise contract requests today; the buyer repeatedly flagged adoption/change management as the make-or-break for rollout.
A planned formal CLM RFP could open a competitive field and push the decision past the buyer's August internal target.
items.source: Avoma CLM Workshop 24 Jun 2026 key takeaways

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warming: recent two-way email, a well-received workshop, and the buyer widening the internal audience toward power and legal.
items: Buyer pushed the recording to VP, General Counsel and three additional senior names (26 Jun to 2 Jul).
Buyer pulled the close date in six months on 23 Jun, signalling intent to move this fiscal cycle.
Working team asked Zycus-specific next steps (budgetary proposal, tailored GC demo, pricing discussion).

scope_change.from: CLM + Merlin Intake + Spend Analytics (as named on the opportunity)
scope_change.to: CLM + Merlin Intake first, with Spend Analytics, Catalog and eSourcing deferred to a later phase
scope_change.detail: Erin Shehadi framed a phased approach on 24 Jun ('It's a phased approach... yes we want Merlin and we want CLM to start') and the Next Step relegates Spend Analytics, Catalog and eSourcing to 'next-phase opportunities', a cost-conscious phasing to watch rather than a big-bang buy.

customer_preference.evidence: Erin Shehadi: 'this is why this is a really important initiative for us... exactly how we're envisioning intake' and 'I love this'; buyer requested a budgetary proposal and a tailored General Counsel demo (Avoma 24 Jun, Clari 26 Jun). Preference is strong at working level but the economic buyer has not weighed in.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (manual intake, ~80% third-party paper reviewed by legal, no policy guidance, sunsetting GEP CLM) but no cost, cycle-time or savings figures; a sizing questionnaire was sent 23 Jun to build the numbers.

items.expected_effect: Puts the legal decision-maker and budget owner in the room, closing the top EARLY-stage power gap and advancing toward the August internal target.

items.expected_effect: Makes deferral more expensive than proceeding and gives the champion numbers to sell upward.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A live, fast-moving CLM replacement where two working-level champions love the platform and the incumbent GEP CLM is sunsetting in our favour; the only thing between us and a strong position is that no economic buyer or General Counsel has yet joined a call.

win_position.text: We are the buyer's leading path on a real replacement: the 24 Jun dry-run workshop landed strongly ('I love this' from Erin Shehadi) and Signet pulled the close date in six months on 23 Jun.
The incumbent GEP CLM is sunsetting by September 2027, a buyer-owned compelling event, and Erin praised our single-platform orchestration as the differentiator GEP lacks.
No economic buyer or General Counsel has met us yet; the deal lives entirely at Manager/Sr. Manager level, so a $350k decision has no confirmed budget sponsor.
A planned formal CLM RFP could re-invite GEP or new vendors on price, and our ROI is still unquantified with the September budget season approaching.

deal_momentum.text: Two-way engagement is recent and rising: workshop 24 Jun, recording shared 29 Jun, access extended to five senior names by 2 Jul, and Rick booking the pricing discussion.
Commercial topics have entered: a budgetary proposal is owed within one to two weeks and a pricing discussion is being scheduled.
The senior names invited to the recording have not yet joined a call, so escalation is intended but not realised.

customer_commitment.text: Signet is investing effort: it shared its MNDA template and workflow, is preparing workflow diagrams for a tailored demo, and is treating the workshop as prep for a General Counsel session.
The buyer wants a phased approach starting with CLM and Merlin Intake, deferring Spend Analytics, Catalog and eSourcing, a cost-conscious signal on the full scope.

deal_risk.text: Economic-buyer VP and General Counsel are unengaged, so power and legal sign-off are unmapped at EARLY stage.
ROI is unquantified and rollout to ~2,000 requesters raises a change-management adoption risk the buyer keeps flagging.

champion_strength.evidence: Two engaged working-level champions (Erin, Megan) but no confirmed access to power or legal.

economic_buyer_access.evidence: VP and General Counsel named by role only, never engaged on a call.

business_case.evidence: Pain clear but ROI unquantified; sizing questionnaire still outstanding.

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won on Signet Jewelers in evidence; a 21 Oct 2025 intro call and prior 2025 procurement relationship exist but no prior win, so executive access is a genuine EARLY-stage gap, not inherited.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Erin Shehadi framed a phased approach on 24 Jun ('It's a phased approach... yes we want Merlin and we want CLM to start') and the Next Step relegates Spend Anal (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 20 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 50; preference strength +1.00 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Erin Shehadi framed a phased approach on 24 Jun ('It's a phased approach... yes we want Merlin and we want CLM….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): commercial topics entering (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 14 is anchored on the stage win (20), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: A live, fast-moving CLM replacement where two working-level champions love the platform and the incumbent GEP CLM is sunsetting in our favour; the only thing between us and a strong position is that no economic buyer or General Counsel has yet joined a call
